title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 41 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: Chinese New Year
festivals.description: Singapore's most vibrant celebration featuring lion dances, festive markets, and family gatherings across the island. The streets of Chinatown come alive with decorations, lanterns, and traditional performances.

festivals.name: Chingay Parade
festivals.description: Asia's largest street performance featuring massive decorated floats, stilt walkers, and elaborate costumes in a multicultural procession. This iconic parade celebrates Chinese, Malay, and Indian cultures together.

festivals.name: Thaipusam
festivals.description: A major Hindu festival where devotees perform ritual acts of devotion, with thousands gathering at Sri Mariamman Temple in Chinatown. The spiritual and colorful celebration showcases Singapore's religious diversity.

festivals.name: Good Friday & Easter
festivals.description: Christian religious observance with church services, community gatherings, and Easter celebrations across Singapore's churches. A public holiday marking the crucifixion and resurrection of Jesus Christ.

festivals.name: Hari Raya Puasa (Eid al-Fitr)
festivals.description: The most important Islamic festival celebrating the end of Ramadan with family feasts, new clothes, and open houses across Muslim communities. Kampong Glam becomes a festive hub with decorations and traditional foods.

festivals.name: Ramadan Night Market (Pasar Ramadan)
festivals.description: Temporary night bazaars throughout Singapore where Muslims and visitors break fast with traditional food, drinks, and festive goods. The markets offer authentic Malay cuisine and cultural immersion.
festivals.location: Various locations including Geylang, Northeast Region

festivals.name: Singapore International Festival of Arts (SIFA)
festivals.description: A world-class arts festival featuring theater, dance, music, and visual arts from international and local artists. The event transforms venues across Marina Bay and the city into dynamic cultural spaces.

festivals.name: Vesak Day
festivals.description: Singapore's major Buddhist festival celebrating the birth, enlightenment, and death of Buddha with temple processions and bathing of Buddha statues. A public holiday marked by spiritual ceremonies and community celebrations.

festivals.name: Dragon Boat Festival
festivals.description: A traditional Chinese festival featuring competitive dragon boat races at East Coast Park with vibrant team costumes and festive atmosphere. The celebration honors ancient Chinese heritage with food and cultural performances.
festivals.location: East Coast Park, East Coast Region

festivals.name: Singapore Food Festival
festivals.description: A month-long celebration of Singapore's diverse culinary heritage featuring hawker stalls, cooking demonstrations, and food tours. This festival showcases the island's multicultural food scene to locals and travelers alike.
festivals.location: Various locations including Chinatown, Central Region

festivals.name: Hari Raya Haji (Eid al-Adha)
festivals.description: The second major Islamic festival honoring Prophet Ibrahim's sacrifice, celebrated with prayers, family gatherings, and festive meals. A public holiday where Muslims and friends gather for communal celebrations.

festivals.name: National Day (August 9th)
festivals.description: Singapore's independence day featuring a spectacular parade at Marina Bay, fireworks, and nationwide celebrations with the national anthem performed by thousands. A powerful display of national pride and unity.

festivals.name: Singapore Night Festival
festivals.description: A dazzling arts and light festival transforming museums and heritage sites around the Bras Basah precinct with colorful projections and performances. The festival celebrates art, culture, and creativity through innovative light displays.
festivals.location: Bras Basah and Bugis, Central Region

festivals.name: Hungry Ghost Festival
festivals.description: A traditional Chinese festival honoring deceased ancestors with street performances, makeshift theaters, and opera shows. Locals and tourists experience unique open-air entertainment and cultural traditions.

festivals.name: Mid-Autumn Festival
festivals.description: A colorful Chinese festival featuring lantern displays, mooncakes, and family celebrations in parks and temples across Singapore. Children parade with traditional lanterns while adults enjoy festive gatherings.

festivals.name: Deepavali (Diwali)
festivals.description: The Festival of Lights celebrating the triumph of light over darkness with oil lamps, colorful decorations, and family gatherings. Little India transforms into a glittering wonderland with temple celebrations and festive markets.

festivals.name: F1 Singapore Grand Prix
festivals.description: The world's first night-time street Formula 1 race featuring high-speed action around Marina Bay's iconic circuit. An electrifying sporting spectacle attracting international visitors and celebrities.

festivals.name: Singapore Writers Festival
festivals.description: An annual literary festival featuring readings, panel discussions, and workshops with renowned international and local authors. A celebration of storytelling and diverse literary voices from across the world.
festivals.location: National Library Building and various venues, Central Region

festivals.name: Christmas Celebrations
festivals.description: A festive season with shopping malls decorated with elaborate displays, holiday markets, and Christmas lights throughout the island. A public holiday celebrated across Singapore's Christian communities and cosmopolitan culture.

festivals.name: New Year's Eve Celebrations
festivals.description: Marina Bay's iconic countdown with massive fireworks display, live performances, and crowded celebrations welcoming the new year. The waterfront becomes the epicenter of festive energy and New Year's revelry.

festivals.name: Singapore Youth Festival
festivals.description: A biennial celebration of youth talent and creativity featuring performances, exhibitions, and competitions across music, dance, and arts. Young performers showcase Singapore's vibrant cultural talents on major stages.

festivals.name: Sentosa Fest
festivals.description: A year-end festival on Sentosa Island featuring live music, food stalls, carnival rides, and entertainment for families. The island becomes a festive playground with diverse attractions and performances.

festivals.name: Lantern Light-Up (Jurong Lake)
festivals.description: Mid-Autumn Festival celebrations at Jurong Lake with thousands of illuminated lanterns and cultural performances. The scenic lakeside becomes a magical setting for family outings and cultural experiences.

festivals.name: Bukit Batok Nature Reserve Heritage Day
festivals.description: An educational celebration of Singapore's natural heritage with guided walks, eco-activities, and conservation talks. Visitors learn about local biodiversity and environmental initiatives.
festivals.location: Bukit Batok Nature Reserve, West Region

festivals.name: Singapore Craft Week
festivals.description: A celebration of traditional and contemporary craftsmanship featuring workshops, exhibitions, and demonstrations by local and international artisans. The week showcases Singapore's creative and maker communities.

festivals.name: Singapore Design Week
festivals.description: An international showcase of design excellence with exhibitions, conferences, and installations featuring designers from around the world. Marina Bay and surrounding areas become hubs of creative innovation.
festivals.location: Marina Bay and various venues, Central Region

festivals.name: Singapore Book Fair
festivals.description: Southeast Asia's largest book event featuring authors, publishers, and book lovers celebrating literature and reading. The fair offers book launches, signings, and literary discussions.

festivals.name: East Coast Carnival
festivals.description: A family-friendly carnival at East Coast Park featuring rides, food stalls, games, and entertainment. The beachside venue offers recreational fun and festive atmosphere.
festivals.location: East Coast Park, East Coast Region

festivals.name: ZoukOut Music Festival
festivals.description: An annual electronic dance music festival on Sentosa's beach featuring international DJs, live performances, and a vibrant nightlife experience. A major event on Singapore's music festival calendar.

festivals.name: Singapore Biennale
festivals.description: A contemporary art exhibition showcasing works from regional and international artists across multiple venues. The biennale explores themes of identity, culture, and artistic expression.

festivals.name: Thimithi Festival
festivals.description: A Hindu festival where devotees walk barefoot over hot coals in a ritual of courage and faith at Sri Mariamman Temple. An ancient tradition showcasing spiritual devotion and cultural heritage.

festivals.name: Singapore Jazz Festival
festivals.description: An annual celebration of jazz music featuring international and local musicians performing across multiple venues. The festival brings world-class jazz performances to Singapore's music lovers.

festivals.name: Lantern Festival at Sungei Serangoon
festivals.description: Mid-Autumn Festival celebrations at Sungei Serangoon with lantern displays, cultural performances, and family activities. The riverside setting creates a serene festive ambiance.

festivals.name: Kranji War Memorial Day
festivals.description: A solemn commemoration honoring those who died in World War II at Kranji War Cemetery with ceremonies and wreaths. The day reflects on historical sacrifice and preserves collective memory.

festivals.name: Singapore Courtesy Week
festivals.description: A nationwide campaign promoting courtesy, kindness, and graciousness among Singaporeans through community events and initiatives. The week celebrates Singapore's values of harmony and mutual respect.

festivals.name: Heritage Festival at Fort Siloso
festivals.description: A celebration of Singapore's military heritage at Fort Siloso featuring living history performances, exhibitions, and interactive displays. Visitors experience wartime history through immersive storytelling.

festivals.name: Sembawang Hot Spring Park Festival
festivals.description: A unique festival celebrating Singapore's natural hot springs at Sembawang with wellness activities, nature walks, and cultural performances. The event highlights the area's geological and ecological significance.
festivals.location: Sembawang Hot Spring Park, Northwest Region

festivals.name: Siloso Beach Carnival
festivals.description: A beachside carnival on Sentosa featuring water sports, beach games, food vendors, and entertainment for all ages. The tropical setting makes it a popular vacation destination for locals and tourists.

festivals.name: Punggol Waterfront Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Punggol's new waterfront development featuring food, music, water activities, and family entertainment. The festival showcases the transformation of Singapore's northeastern coast.

festivals.name: Lunar New Year at Universal Studios Singapore
festivals.description: Special themed celebrations at Universal Studios featuring zodiac characters, festive shows, and limited-edition dining. The theme park brings Lunar New Year magic with entertainment and attractions.

festivals.name: Gardens by the Bay Supertree Light Show
festivals.description: A nightly spectacular featuring illuminated Supertrees with synchronized music and lights throughout the year, enhanced during festive seasons. The iconic display is a signature Singapore experience combining nature and technology.
festivals.location: Gardens by the Bay, Marina Bay
